home.title: Run {em} Not Admin.
home.subtitle: Tornare is your operations cockpit for Overwatch communities. Launch events quickly, keep signups visible, and move from planning to lobby with less friction.

home.jumpNewsDesc: Broadcast patch notes, rule changes, and league news to everyone in one feed.
home.jumpAboutDesc: See the roadmap and mission behind Tornare and where the platform is headed next.
home.bannerTitle: Command Center For Captains And Organizers
home.bannerDesc: From signup links to team coordination, Tornare keeps your event lifecycle visible and actionable.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers across events, players, teams, and accounts.
faq.footerText: Didn't find what you were looking for?
faq.s1q1: How do I create an account?
faq.s1a1: Click "Sign in" in the top navigation. You can register with a username and password, or link an existing Discord or Battle.net account via OAuth on the Auth page.
faq.s1q2: How do I create an event?
faq.s1a2: Open the Events page and use the "Create Event" button in the header. You will be prompted to name the event, configure its format, and set signup options. Newly created events start in Draft status.
faq.s1q3: How do I invite players to my event?
faq.s1a3: From the event's Requests tab, copy the signup link and share it in your community Discord or chat. Players do not need a Tornare account to submit a signup — they just open the link and fill in the form.
faq.s1a4: When public registration is enabled, anyone with the signup link can request to join. When disabled, only manually approved players can access the event. Toggle this in the event's Settings tab.
faq.s2a1: The balancing engine distributes players across teams by optimising role coverage (Tank, Support, DPS) and minimising total rank ELO difference between teams. Ensure all players have a rank set before running the balance for best results.
faq.s2q2: Can I manually adjust teams after auto-balance?
faq.s2a2: Yes. After running auto-balance, any team slot can be edited individually from the Teams section of the event.
faq.s2q3: Can a player update their signup after submitting?
faq.s2a3: Players cannot edit a submitted request directly. As an organiser you can reject and re-invite them, which lets them submit a fresh request with updated information.
faq.s2q4: What are the different event statuses?
faq.s2a4: Draft — being set up; not visible to the public, signups not accepted. Active — live and visible; players can submit signup requests if Public Registration is enabled. Ended — event is over; still visible in Past Events but locked for new signups.
faq.s2q5: Can I archive or soft-delete an event?
faq.s2a5: Yes. From the event Settings tab you can mark an event as deleted. It remains in the database for record-keeping but is hidden from the public events list. Admins can still access it.
faq.s3a1: Tornare currently supports Tank, Support, and DPS — the standard Overwatch 2 role categories. Players can set primary and secondary role preferences when submitting a signup request.
faq.s3q2: What are ranks and how is ELO calculated?
faq.s3a2: Players self-report their competitive rank (e.g. Gold 3, Platinum 1). Tornare maps these to numeric ELO values used for team balancing. The tiers follow the standard Overwatch competitive ladder.
faq.s3a3: Flex indicates the player has no strong role preference and is willing to fill any position. The balance engine uses flex players as wildcards when optimising team compositions.
faq.s3q4: Can a player be assigned a different role than their preference?
faq.s3a4: Yes. Organisers can override the assigned role for any player in the Teams section. The player's preference is shown as a hint, but the final assignment is always under organiser control.
faq.s4q1: How do I link my Discord account?
faq.s4a1: Go to your Profile page and click "Link Discord". You will be redirected to Discord's OAuth screen. After authorising, your Discord username will appear on your profile and on your player cards in events.
faq.s4q2: How do I link my Battle.net account?
faq.s4a2: The same flow applies: Profile → "Link Battle.net" → authorise on Blizzard's site. Your BattleTag will then be linked to your Tornare identity and shown on player cards.
faq.s4a3: Username changes are not currently self-served. Contact support if you need a correction. Display names can be updated freely from your Profile settings at any time.
faq.s4q4: How do I delete my account?
faq.s4a4: Send a deletion request through the Support page. We will anonymise your personal data within 30 days. Event participation records are retained in anonymised form to preserve tournament history.

about.heroSub: Tornare started from a simple friction point: community organizers spending more time on admin than on the game itself.
about.quote: "The focus is on practical execution, from first signup to final match handoff."
about.focusDesc: Reduce setup friction from signup to lobby handoff. We automate the repetitive tasks so you can focus on the community.
about.approachDesc: Clear states for roster, teams, and match progression. Never guess where a tournament stands again.
about.outcomeDesc: Less admin overhead, more energy on gameplay quality. Higher player retention and smoother execution.
about.integrityDesc: Built for organizers who run serious community game nights. We handle the heavy lifting of tournament logistics.
about.pillar1Text: Every event phase should be visible: setup, signup, balancing, and final match execution without tab-switching.
about.pillar2Text: Common actions should take seconds, not context switches across spreadsheets and chat threads.
about.pillar3Text: Tools should support captains, hosts, and players equally with transparent and accessible event data.
about.roadmapDesc: We are building the future of community tournament management. Here is what we are working on right now and what is coming next.
about.roadmapNowItems: Event setup, profile system, role-aware team management, and polished list experiences.
about.roadmapNextItems: OAuth identity linking, richer player analytics, and deeper organizer automation flows.
about.roadmapLaterItems: League-level tooling, historical performance dashboards, and expanded ecosystem integrations.
about.principle1: Prefer obvious workflows over hidden controls.
about.principle2: Make ownership boundaries explicit for safe collaboration.
about.principle3: Expose useful status signals early so organizers can act quickly.
about.principlesQuote: "Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication in operations management."

news.introCopy: Follow feature drops, database migrations, and community announcements as Tornare ships toward a full public release.

support.title: Support
support.subtitle: Get help with events, accounts, and platform issues.
support.discordDesc: Fastest path to help. Post in #support and the community or a maintainer will respond.
support.bugsDesc: Found a reproducible issue? Open a GitHub issue with steps and any visible error message.
support.contactDesc: For account, privacy, or data requests that require private handling.
support.topic1Desc: Make sure the event is published (status ACTIVE) and "Public Registration" is enabled in the Settings tab before sharing the signup link. Players cannot submit requests while the event is a Draft or after it has Ended.
support.topic2Desc: If a player cannot submit a request, verify the event is public and the link is current. Reject + re-invite resets their request.
support.topic3Desc: Balancing uses rank ELO and role targets. Ensure all players have a rank set before running auto-balance for best results.
support.topic4Desc: Linking Discord or Battle.net requires completing the OAuth flow from your Profile settings page. Check browser pop-up blockers if it fails.
support.bugReportIntro: Include all of the following to get the fastest resolution:
support.bugItem1Strong: What you were trying to do
support.bugItem1: — a one-sentence description of the intended action.
support.bugItem2: — a numbered, repeatable sequence from a clean state.
support.bugItem3: — the result, including any error text shown on screen.
support.bugItem4: — copy-paste from the browser address bar.
support.responseDiscordDesc: Usually a few hours during active community times. Best for general questions.
support.responsePrivacyDesc: We respond within 30 days as required by applicable law. Email preferred for privacy matters.
support.responseBugDesc: Tracked on GitHub. High-impact bugs are prioritised in the next release cycle.

legal.privacyIntro: Tornare ("we", "us", or "our") operates the Tornare platform, a community tournament management tool. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and what your rights are. By using Tornare, you agree to the practices described here.
legal.privacyS1P1: When you register, we collect a username, an email address, and a hashed password. If you link a third-party identity (Discord or Battle.net), we additionally store your platform username and a stable provider identifier. We never store your third-party passwords or access tokens beyond the duration of a session.
legal.privacyS1P2: You may optionally provide a display name and a profile avatar. Avatars are restricted to preset images hosted on our domain and are never uploaded from your device.
legal.privacyS1P3: We record the events you create, your signup requests (name, selected roles, rank self-assessment, and any voluntarily supplied contact handles), team assignments, and match results. This information is necessary for the service to function.
legal.privacyS1P4: We may collect standard server logs including IP addresses, HTTP request paths, and timestamps for the purposes of security monitoring, abuse prevention, and performance diagnosis. These logs are retained for a maximum of 90 days.
legal.privacyS2L1D: — Powering event creation, signup management, team balancing, and match scheduling.
legal.privacyS2L2D: — Verifying your identity, detecting suspicious activity, and sending security-critical notifications.
legal.privacyS2L3D: — Sending transactional messages related to your account or events you are participating in. We do not send marketing emails without explicit consent.
legal.privacyS2L4D: — Aggregated, anonymised usage patterns may be analysed to improve product quality. This analysis does not identify individuals.
legal.privacyS3P1: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to third parties.
legal.privacyS3P2: We may disclose information in the following limited circumstances:
legal.privacyS3L1D: — Our cloud infrastructure provider processes data on our behalf under a data processing agreement.
legal.privacyS3L2D: — If required by applicable law, court order, or governmental authority.
legal.privacyS3L3D: — To investigate, prevent, or act against fraud, abuse, or violations of these policies.
legal.privacyS3P3: Event data that you mark as public (event name, date, format, and results) is visible to other users of the platform. Signup contact handles (Discord tag, Battle.net tag) are only visible to event owners and administrators.
legal.privacyS4P1: We retain your account data for as long as your account remains active. Event and participation records are retained indefinitely to preserve community history. You may request deletion of your account and associated personal data at any time; event records will be anonymised rather than deleted to preserve tournament history integrity.
legal.privacyS5P1a: Tornare stores your authentication tokens (access token and refresh token) in your browser's
legal.privacyS5P1b: . No session cookies are used for authentication. No advertising or analytics cookies are set. We do not use any third-party tracking pixels or fingerprinting scripts.
legal.privacyS6P1: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights regarding your personal information:
legal.privacyS6L1D: — Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
legal.privacyS6L2D: — Correct inaccurate or incomplete data via your profile settings.
legal.privacyS6L3D: — Request erasure of your account and personal data.
legal.privacyS6L4D: — Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
legal.privacyS6L5D: — Object to processing based on legitimate interests.
legal.privacyS6P2: To exercise any of these rights, contact us through the Support page. We will respond within 30 days.
legal.privacyS7P1: Tornare is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe we have inadvertently collected such information, please contact us immediately and we will delete it promptly.
legal.privacyS8P1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we do, we will revise the "Last updated" date at the top of this page and, for material changes, post a notice on the platform. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
legal.privacyS9P1a: For privacy questions, data requests, or concerns, please reach out through the
legal.termsIntro: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the Tornare platform. Please read them carefully. By creating an account or using any part of the service, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use Tornare.
legal.termsS1P1: You must be at least 13 years old to use Tornare. If you are under 18, you represent that you have your parent or guardian's permission. By using the service you warrant that you meet these requirements and that you are not prohibited from using the service under applicable laws.
legal.termsS2P1: You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account. You must provide accurate information when registering and must keep your credentials secure and confidential. You may not share your account with others or create accounts for the purpose of abuse, impersonation, or circumventing suspensions.
legal.termsS2P2: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms, without prior notice, at our sole discretion.
legal.termsS3P1: You agree to use Tornare only for lawful purposes. You must not:
legal.termsS3L1: Post, transmit, or distribute content that is unlawful, harassing, defamatory, obscene, or otherwise objectionable.
legal.termsS3L2: Impersonate any person or entity, or misrepresent your affiliation with any person or entity.
legal.termsS3L3: Attempt to gain unauthorised access to any part of the service, other accounts, or connected systems.
legal.termsS3L4: Use automated scripts, bots, or other tools to scrape, crawl, or abuse the platform.
legal.termsS3L5: Interfere with the integrity or performance of the service or the data it contains.
legal.termsS3L6: Use the service to facilitate cheating, match-fixing, or fraudulent competitive outcomes.
legal.termsS4P1: By submitting content to Tornare (including event names, player names, match results, and profile information), you grant us a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free licence to store, display, and distribute that content solely for the purpose of operating and improving the platform. You retain ownership of your content.
legal.termsS4P2: You are solely responsible for the accuracy of the content you submit. We do not verify the accuracy of event data or competitive results.
legal.termsS5P1: Tornare allows you to link third-party accounts (Discord, Battle.net). These integrations are provided for convenience. Your use of those third-party services is governed by their respective terms and privacy policies, not ours. We are not responsible for any data practices of those services.
legal.termsS6P1: The Tornare name, logo, interface design, and all platform software are the exclusive property of Tornare and are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You may not copy, modify, or distribute any part of the platform without our prior written consent.
legal.termsS6P2: Game titles, character names, and related assets referenced on this platform (including Overwatch) are the property of their respective owners. Tornare is an independent community tool and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Blizzard Entertainment.
legal.termsS7P1: Tornare is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. We do not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or free of harmful components.
legal.termsS8P1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Tornare and its operators shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of or inability to use the service, even if advised of the possibility of such damages. Our total liability for any claim arising under these Terms shall not exceed the amount you paid us in the twelve months preceding the claim (which, for a free service, is zero).
legal.termsS9P1: We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue the service (or any part thereof) at any time with or without notice. We will not be liable to you or any third party for any modification, suspension, or discontinuation of the service.
legal.termsS10P1: We may revise these Terms from time to time. When we do, we will update the "Last updated" date and, for material changes, post a notice on the platform. Your continued use of Tornare after changes take effect constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms.
legal.termsS11P1: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which the platform is operated, without regard to conflict of law provisions. Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of that jurisdiction.
